docker-6._vuln_deploy_note: Real files now provided (Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml). This is a two-container CVE: 'apache' (build: ., the vulnerable mod_proxy/AJP front door, port 8080->80) reverse-proxies via AJP to a plain 'tomcat' backend (vulhub/tomcat:8.5.19, no exposed port -- only reachable through apache's ProxyPass). Only apache needed container_name for the role's confirm-running check; tomcat got one too (tomcat-backend-cve-2021-40438) purely for operator clarity, not required by any role logic.

docker-10._flag_node_note: Not a CVE -- this host runs a small custom TCP service (files/git-deploy-key-repo/) implementing exactly the AUTH/LIST/GET protocol and values from the XML's git_deploy_key_repo flag-node-generator. Deployed via topology-flagnodes.yml (new), reusing docker/vuln_node unmodified since it doesn't care whether the directory it deploys is a real CVE or not.

topology._topology_note: No routers, no r2r links, no routing plan, no traffic flows in this scenario's XML (routers: [], routing_plan: {}, r2r_subnets: [], Traffic section explicit_count=0/weight_sum=0). All 10 docker hosts sit on one flat switch (sw-11, 172.23.255.0/24) with no router in between -- topology-routers.yml/topology-links.yml/topology-routing.yml/topology-traffic.yml are all omitted from playbooks.yml for this scenario rather than included as no-ops.

hitl._note: XML has HardwareInLoop enabled=true, attachment='existing_router' on ens19 (10.0.0.21/24) -- but this topology has zero routers, so there's nothing for it to attach to. Per your decision, HITL is skipped entirely for this scenario (treated as disabled) rather than adding a router just to host it. topology-hitl.yml omitted from playbooks.yml.
